text: The surgeon demonstrates a laparoscopic suture technique during a urethrovesicular anastomosis. A right-hand suture is placed at the six o'clock position using a monocryl euro one needle. The suture is then passed through the urethra from the inside out with right-handed forward rotation bites. A left-hand bite is placed from eleven o'clock to five o'clock, followed by a right-hand bite at three o'clock. The non-braided monofilament suture is pulled, and a reverse bite is performed with the right hand. The left-hand suture is placed from nine o'clock to three o'clock, followed by a right-hand suture from nine o'clock to three o'clock on the patient's right side.
